title: Loan disbursement without complete KYC documentation
description: Loans disbursed to customers without obtaining all mandatory KYC documents as per RBI KYC Master Direction 2016. Risk of regulatory penalty and potential money laundering exposure.
regulatory_reference: RBI KYC Master Direction 2016; PMLA 2002 S.12
existing_controls: Pre-disbursement KYC checklist; Ops team spot-checks; system KYC flag
treatment_action_plan: 1. Implement system-enforced KYC gate before disbursement (IT - 60 days). 2. Retro-verify open cases. 3. Mandatory training for loan officers.
cro_comment: Critical process gap. Escalated to CCO. System fix prioritised.

title: Non-submission of half-yearly returns to NABARD
description: Half-yearly statutory returns (NBS-7 equivalent for cooperative banks) not submitted to NABARD within prescribed timeline. Risk of NABARD supervisory action and potential licence conditions.
regulatory_reference: NABARD Circular DCBS.CO.RCBD.BC No.16; State Cooperative Societies Act
existing_controls: Compliance calendar maintained; CCO reviews quarterly
treatment_action_plan: 1. Implement automated compliance calendar with email alerts (30 days). 2. Assign dedicated staff for NABARD reporting. 3. CCO to sign off on all regulatory submissions.
cro_comment: Awaiting CCO confirmation of revised compliance calendar.

title: High NPA concentration in MSME lending segment
description: MSME loan portfolio showing NPA ratio of 8.2% against threshold of 5%. Concentration in textile sector (32% of MSME book) exacerbates risk during economic downturn.
regulatory_reference: RBI IRAC Norms; Basel III Pillar 2
existing_controls: Sector exposure limits in credit policy; quarterly NPA review; provisioning as per IRAC
treatment_action_plan: 1. Impose sector cap: textile exposure ≤ 20% of MSME book within 6 months. 2. Strengthen collections for NPA accounts. 3. Review credit appraisal criteria for MSME segment.
cro_comment: Reviewed. Board informed. Sector cap approved at BRC meeting Jan 2025.

title: Core banking system downtime affecting member transactions
description: CBS (Core Banking Software) experienced 3 unplanned outages in Q3 FY25, total downtime 11 hours. Member transactions could not be processed; manual workarounds inadequate. NABARD IT audit finding pending.
regulatory_reference: RBI IT Framework for Banks; NABARD IT Circular 2020
existing_controls: Basic DR site exists; manual transaction register maintained at branches
treatment_action_plan: 1. Upgrade CBS to cloud-hosted version with 99.9% SLA (6 months). 2. Conduct DR drill quarterly. 3. Close NABARD IT audit finding with action plan.

title: Phishing attack on collections team — credential compromise
description: 3 collections staff credentials compromised via phishing email. Attacker accessed customer loan data for approximately 2,400 accounts. Incident reported to CERT-In within 6 hours per RBI Cyber Framework.
regulatory_reference: RBI Cyber Security Framework 2016; CERT-In Directions 2022; IT Act 2000
existing_controls: Email filtering (basic); annual cyber awareness training
treatment_action_plan: 1. Force password reset for all affected accounts (Done). 2. Deploy advanced email security with AI phishing detection (45 days). 3. Mandatory monthly phishing simulation for all staff. 4. Notify affected customers per DPDP Act.
cro_comment: P1 Cyber Incident. CRO, MD & CEO notified. BRC briefed. RBI reporting done.

title: ALM mismatch — short-term liabilities funding long-term assets
description: Significant ALM mismatch identified: 42% of liabilities are due within 3 months while only 18% of assets mature within the same period. Stress scenario shows liquidity shortfall of ₹85 Crore under moderate stress.
regulatory_reference: RBI Guidelines on Liquidity Risk Management 2012; LCR Requirements
existing_controls: Monthly ALM committee; LCR monitoring; RBI liquidity reporting
treatment_action_plan: 1. Increase proportion of term deposits > 1 year (target: +15% within 2 quarters). 2. Review loan product tenure mix. 3. Establish contingent liquidity facility with correspondent bank.
cro_comment: Approved. ALCO action plan accepted. Monthly monitoring mandatory.

title: Ombudsman complaint — mis-selling of recurring deposit scheme
description: Banking Ombudsman received 14 complaints from senior citizen members regarding mis-selling of a recurring deposit scheme — customers claim they were not informed of premature withdrawal penalties. Local media coverage.
regulatory_reference: RBI Fair Practices Code; Banking Ombudsman Scheme 2006; Consumer Protection Act 2019
existing_controls: Product disclosure form signed by customers; branch manager oversight
treatment_action_plan: 1. Respond to all Ombudsman complaints within 30 days. 2. Retrain branch staff on fair practices. 3. Revise product disclosure form. 4. CCO to review all deposit product documentation.

title: Unhedged forex exposure on ECB drawdown
description: External Commercial Borrowing (ECB) of USD 10 million drawn in Q3 FY25. Only 60% hedged via forward contracts. Remaining USD 4 million unhedged, exposing the institution to INR depreciation risk.
regulatory_reference: RBI ECB Framework; FEMA 1999; RBI Hedging Guidelines
existing_controls: Board-approved forex policy; 60% hedge ratio currently maintained
treatment_action_plan: 1. Increase hedge ratio to minimum 80% within 30 days. 2. Review forex policy to set 80% minimum as mandatory. 3. Monthly forex exposure reporting to ALCO.
cro_comment: Hedge ratio improvement noted. Treasury to report monthly to CRO.

title: Key person dependency — single credit approver for MSME
description: All MSME credit approvals above ₹50 Lakh routed through a single credit officer. No documented delegation of authority or trained backup. Business continuity risk if this individual is unavailable.
regulatory_reference: RBI Guidelines on Operational Risk; Internal Succession Policy
existing_controls: Informal handoff to CRO when credit officer is on leave
treatment_action_plan: 1. Identify and train 2 backup credit approvers (90 days). 2. Document Delegation of Authority matrix. 3. Cross-train MSME and retail credit teams.

title: AML transaction monitoring — manual process gap
description: AML transaction monitoring conducted manually using Excel — system-based monitoring not implemented. Risk of suspicious transactions going unreported within PMLA timelines. NABARD raised this as a finding in the last audit.
regulatory_reference: PMLA 2002; RBI AML/KYC Master Direction; NABARD AML Circular
existing_controls: Manual review of large-value transactions; STR reporting process exists
treatment_action_plan: 1. Procure and implement automated AML software within 6 months. 2. Interim: increase frequency of manual monitoring to weekly. 3. Train compliance team on PMLA obligations.
cro_comment: Critical compliance gap. Approved for priority budget allocation. Monthly progress to CRO.

title: Absence of tested BCP / DR plan for digital lending platform

regulatory_reference: RBI IT Framework for NBFCs 2017; RBI BCP Guidelines
existing_controls: Basic server backup; informal recovery steps documented
treatment_action_plan: 1. Develop comprehensive BCP/DR plan for digital platform (90 days). 2. Conduct DR drill within 120 days. 3. Annual DR drill to be institutionalised.

title: Inadequate loan loss provisioning — IRAC non-compliance
